title: Level 428
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|date=June 28, 2026|1=Generally needs a better execution}} __NOTOC__
{{Background|file=PlumPathway1.png}}
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 428: "Plum Pathways"'''}}{{Class Custom Image|hexagon=1|bg-image=Plum.png|text-color=white|class=Plum|safety=Safe|security=Unstable|entity=Devoid of Entities}} '''Level 428''', also known as the '''"Plum Pathways"''', is the 429th level of the Backrooms. {{Blurimage|file=PlumPathway1.png|caption=A stable version of the hallway in Level 428.}}''' == Overview == '''Level 428''' consists of an infinitely repeating hallway, which in turn comprises a gray tiled floor with black and white stripes, fluorescent yellow and purple lights which are spread around the ceiling, pillars that assist in stabilizing the ceiling, and assorted plastic furniture of various bright and vivid colors scattered randomly across the hall. The walls of the hallway are composed of small, reflective ceramic tiles. At the end of every hallway, there exists a strange bright white light which leads to the next iteration of the hallway, which is quite a unique feature as many other levels consisting of long corridors which repeat ad infinitum simply blend seamlessly into their respective corridor's following iteration. Every iteration of the hallway is subtly different, even in the slightest possible alterations, such as a piece of furniture changing location or the fluorescent lights dimming ever so slightly. On every walk through the hallway, there is always a notably huge mural on the wall which stays in the same location no matter which iteration of the corridor one is present within, albeit this immobility is not true of the metal door on the opposite wall. Due to '''Level 428''' posting wanderers to random hallway iterations every time they step into the light, wanderers in the level are generally unable to form groups, and those who miraculously managed to meet up with another have found themselves separated as soon as they stepped through the blinding light. This is the main cause of there being no extant colonies in the level, amongst other issues. The colorful furniture changes positions depending on the iteration of the hallway, and traversing through the level for a long enough spell may cause the furniture to clip into illogical places, such as glitching into walls, levitating, and such (though these anomalies is also affected by how stable that particular corridor happens to be). Ever and anon, objects can be found on these furniture items, typically the chairs, though there seems to be no logic behind how frequently these items appear, it's only that objects are extremely uncommon. Regularly, objects found on furniture are purple [[Object 36|cooked Slime Organ]], [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]], [[Object 39|Cubes of Transportation]], and most commonly and intriguingly, '''plums'''. Regardless of the positions of the furniture, the colors of the furniture are limited and seemingly signify the relative stability of the corridor they are present within. Wanderers have noted down the supposed meanings of these furniture colors. {| class="fandom-table" |+Furniture Colors !Color !Rarity !Meaning |- |'''White''' |Extremely common, occurs often when you walk through the hallway. |No connotations of stability. |- |'''Black''' |Less common than white, but still common. |Likewise, no connotations of stability. |- |'''Light Orange''' |Decently common, and occurs quite frequently. |Hallway is extremely stable. |- |'''Light Red''' |Decently common, though occurs slightly less than light orange. |Hallway's stability is high. |- |'''Green''' |Somewhat rare, happening every few walks through the hallway. |Hallway's stability is satisfactory, albeit glitching may rarely occur. |- |'''Blue''' |Very rare, usually once in twenty walks through the hallway. |Hallway's stability is lower than satisfactory. |- |'''Plum''' |This color's existence has not been confirmed. Supposedly extremely rare. |Wanderers suppose this indicates that the hallway's stability is alarmingly low. |} Interestingly, no photos of exceedingly unstable hallways have been taken nor found due to how dangerous they are, although certain research groups hold out hope that [[Entity 1|the Cameraman]] has been able to capture a photograph of one. {{Blurimage|file=PlumPathway2.png|caption=A photo of a similar stable hallway, closer to the Nexus.}} === The Nexus === '''The Nexus''' is a metal double door with curved stainless steel handles which may appear at random on the rightward wall of hallways in '''Level 428'''. Going through the door leads to a suffocating darkness, before one is inexplicably sent to another level. The amount of objects the wanderer is carrying delays the teleport by roughly a minute each, and this delay also applies to the number of wanderers. As for the impermeable darkness beyond the door, even with using multiple light sources, the room remains dark. Strangely, bringing '''plums''' into the door temporarily changes the shadows from midnight black to a dark '''plum'''. This, however, seemingly does not alter the effects of '''the Nexus'''. == Entities == This level is devoid of entities whatsoever. == Colonies == As finding other wanderers is extremely difficult due to the randomness and sheer size of the level, no colonies have been found nor heard of. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * Finding a window with a '''plum''' light in [[Level 188]] and entering it can lead one here. * Interacting with a gray arcade machine when the screen flickers '''plum''' in [[Level 25]] can lead one here. === Exits === * In very unstable iterations of the hallway, pressing hard on one of the wall's reflective tiles can lead one to [[Level 33]]. * Teleporting with a [[Object 39|Cube of Transportation]] at the same time that '''the Nexus''' performs its transportation will send one to [[Level 115]]. * Going through '''the Nexus''' and waiting will send one to a random level. == Addendum ==

{{Fullscreen|number=1|show='''> FILE-001'''|hide=> Back|padding=30px|content= {{Background|file=PlumBG.jpeg}}
'''FILE-001'''
'''"Plums"''' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} == Overview == '''Object 428''', also known as '''plums''', are fruits of some species in the subgenus '''''Prunus'' subg. ''Prunus''''', which grow abundantly in [[the Frontrooms]] and may be found in certain areas of [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]], especially '''Level 428'''. Although considered a completely regular and unremarkable fruit to most, it has been revealed that '''plums''' present in '''Level 428''' propagate strange anomalies that may cause various instabilities within the level. This is most evident in that bringing '''plums''' into the door known as '''the Nexus''' may cause its shadowy murk to lighten into a dark plum shade, and though it was once believed that no other anomalous effects were bestowed to the level upon this action, this notion has since been disproven. Recent research conducted by the [[Backroom Colonists]] has revealed that the bringing of '''plums''' into '''the Nexus''' is actually causing a growing instability within the level. Various meticulous tests and experiments conducted show that, every time a wanderer exits via this metal door whilst having pocketed one of these purplish fruits, a corridor somewhere in the level will experience a sudden sharp decrease in stability, causing the color of its furniture to deteriorate into one representative of its new relative stability level as well. This creates the horrifying notion that, if wanderers keep on exiting the level with '''plums''' in hand, the entire level will one day reach a point of such vast instability that it will effectively crumble from within and thus cease to exist. Despite this painstaking research, however, the [[Backroom Colonists]] (nor any other group, for that matter), have been able to determine the exact whys and wherefores of the '''plum''''s innate and unique peculiarity within '''Level 428''' specifically. Other fruits in the subgenus '''''Prunus'' subg. ''Prunus''''' have been tested in a similar fashion, yet only those which are scientifically considered "'''plums'''" seem to serve any purpose in lowering stability; such relatives as apricots and bush cherries seem to have no effect on altering the level's nature at all, only serving to delay '''the Nexus''''s teleportation as most items do. Experiments involving certain fruits in a sister subgenus, ''Prunus'' subg. ''Amygdalus'', have also been conducted, yet only one of the candidates seemed to have any effect on stability at all; this being the almond, which slightly improved stability when brought through '''the Nexus''', albeit in a much smaller ratio compared to '''plums'''. However, many researchers have been tempted to dismiss this as a side effect of the [[Object 1|anomalous properties]] almonds innately possess in the Backrooms as a whole. [[File:Plums.jpg|400px|thumb|center]]
}} {{Fullscreen|number=2|show='''> FILE-002'''|hide=> Back|padding=30px|content= {{Background|file=Void.png}}
'''FILE-002'''
'''03-11-2021''' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ''[[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]].'' ''The largest blackout the Backrooms has seen in forever.'' ''It has been known to destabilize many levels... The classics: [[Level 7]], [[Level Fun]], even the infamous [[Level !]]...'' ''But none so much as '''Level 428'''.'' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ''(The following is a transcript of a recording made by two members of the [[Backroom Colonists]], whose names have been expunged for the sake of remaining anonymous.)'' '''Subject-001:''' Where... are we? '''Subject-002:''' I... I think we've been here before. The higher-ups [[Backroom Colonists|back home]], they call it... the '''Plum Pathways'''. '''Subject-001:''' Are you sure? I thought the '''Plum Pathways''' were... well, plum. The walls, the ceilings, everything here is white. [REDACTED], we can't be sure that this is— '''Subject-002:''' Of course we can. See the painting on the right wall? That is, I think, definite proof of where we are. Yes, the level was originally plum-colored, but... we're in the midst of [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]]. Many levels are being affected, and the loss of color from these walls may be one of them. '''Subject-001:''' Speaking of [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]] — it's so unnaturally bright here. Shouldn't the blackout have, well, blacked the level out? But this level has, if anything, gotten even brighter since our group's last expedition here? '''Subject-002:''' That may just be a result of the lighting turning from plum to white, but... I agree, something seems off here... The furniture is ''floating''. '''Subject-001:''' Hey, is that [[Object 6|moth jelly]]? And... a plum? Since when did the '''Plum Pathways''' spawn ''food''? '''Subject-002:''' Don't get distracted, [REDACTED]. We should try to get back to [[Level_1#Backroom_Colonists_Base_Blink|Base Blink]] as quickly as possible. We never should've gotten separated from— '''Subject-001:''' Wait. I think we should at least bring some specimens of these objects back, as proof to the higher-ups that the level has changed. ''('''Subject-001''' grabs several plums and a jar of [[Object 6|moth jelly]] from a dark blue plastic chair.)'' '''Subject-002:''' ''(sighing)'' You do you, I suppose. Come on, let's get on with it. ''(The two step into '''the Nexus''' and exit the level. However, just before they are teleported, the hallway from which they had just entered shudders, and they watch in horror as it crumbles before their eyes. And then they are gone.)''
}} {{Fullscreen|number=3|show='''> FILE-003'''|hide=> Back|padding=30px|content=

{{CSS|content= .fullscreen { height: 100vh; width: 100vw; object-fit: cover; position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; } }}
'''FILE-003'''
'''"The Doomsday Clock"''' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} [[File:DoomsdayClock.jpg|400px|thumb|center]] {{Underline|FFFFFF}} == Timeline == === The succulent plum, as when it first falls from its tree... === Many years ago in 2011, when the [[M.E.G.]] first discovered the level, it was ever bathed in a plum light; all the furniture was in the same plum color, and no foodstuffs or objects of any kind appeared on them; and every iteration of the hallway was the same, with no differing levels of stability whatsoever. The only thing not of a plum colour in the level was the bright white light at either end of each corridor, leading into the next iteration; this is also an aspect that has not altered across the many years of the '''Plum Pathways'''' existence. '''The Nexus''', too, was far more stable, and did not cause the stability deterioration of hallway iterations when plums were brought through it; nor did the amount of objects or people entering it delay the amount of time it took for its transportation. Indeed, the metal door once acted as a sort of [[Object 39|Cube of Transportation]], allowing its users to select which level they ended up in, albeit with the occasional malfunction. These were the best days of '''Level 428''', known informally as '''the Succulent Plum'''. === The dehydrated plum, as when its water begins to dwindle... === Ever since [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]], '''Level 428''' has not been the same. As of 2021, it has been discovered that the Backrooms-wide incident affected the '''Plum Pathways''' in a way never before seen — instead of entering a period of suffocating darkness like many of the other levels known to wanderers, '''Level 428''' instead became brighter, losing its anomalous plum tint for a regular, stark white light. However, this is not the only way in which the level was affected during this period, known colloqiually as '''the Dehydrated Plum'''. Like many more noteworthy levels, certain areas of '''Level 428''' were heavily destabilized during [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]], and thus the somewhat coherent 'system' of furniture color to determine stability was implemented. This is also around the time when '''the Nexus''' began to affect stability if plums were brought into it, which, interestingly, coincides with when plums first began to be spawned in the level. It has been documented that '''the Nexus''' was extremely insecure at this time, with even a single plum brought into it causing the instant deterioration — and, on occasion, destruction — of many stable iterations of the hallway. === The shrivelled prune, as when it has become dried and soulless... === In around 2023, around when the Surge began to bring light back to the Backrooms, the stability levels of '''the Nexus''' began to creep back up in a period known as '''the Shrivelled Prune'''. Wanderers could now safely bring plums through the metal door without simultaneously destroying multiple instances of '''Level 428''''s hallway, this action instead causing the mere deterioration of a few hallways' relative stability level. However, research conducted by the [[Backroom Colonists]] shows that multiple corridors are still crumbling into dust every day due to the massive amount of wanderers exiting the level through '''the Nexus''', and hence it is believed that the end is both nigh and inevitable. === The bloated prune, as when its tenders try and fail to restore its succulence... === And now is entered the realm of speculation and hypotheticals. The [[Backroom Colonists]] have theorized that the '''Plum Pathways''' will slowly regain stability, as they have already begun to do; yet it is believed that the level shall never fully recover from the dastardly effects of [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]], meaning that — although this short window of time may drastically decrease the amount of hallways collapsed or destabilized — it will likely never regain the plum lighting that used to tint it, nor will '''the Nexus''' ever return to its old status as a device with abilities and stability equivalent to [[Object 39|Cubes of Transportation]] again. Hence, instances of corridors in '''Level 428''' will still continue to dissolve, albeit at a much slower rate. This hypothetical period of time has been dubbed by the [[Backroom Colonists|Colonists]] as '''the Bloated Prune''', supposed to take place from late 2025 to approximately 2034. === ...and then it all came to an end. === Thus comes the time when no more iterations of '''Level 428''''s hallway will be extant anymore, for they will all have crumbled from bringing of plums into '''the Nexus'''; and the level will effectively cease to exist. The only remnants of this level that will still lay out there somewhere, it is hypothesized, is '''the Nexus''' itself — and this has already been seen; for those who have escaped [[the Void]], where time and space are meaningless, have reported seeing a set of metal double doors floating in the eternal abyss. The great hypothesists of the [[Backroom Colonists]] know this event simply as '''Doomsday''', and the course of time over which it draws ever nearer as '''the Doomsday Clock'''; at the current rate of '''Nexus'''-usage, '''Doomsday''' is predicted to occur during approximately late 2034 or early 2035. However, researchers believe that if wanderers manage to greatly reduce the amount of plums brought into '''the Nexus''' by at least half of the current rate, the cataclysmic event could be postponed until around 2051. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ''Eighty-nine seconds until midnight...'' {{Background|file=Void.png}} }}
